summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a malicious plugin can use extendContext to pollute the Object prototype via Object.assign, causing newly created objects to inherit polluted properties that could enable privilege escalation.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that empty objects created after the malicious plugin executes inherit properties from the polluted Object prototype, specifically isAdmin=true and polluted='PROTOTYPE_POLLUTED'.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, executed the malicious plugin, and confirmed that empty objects (testObject, anotherObject) inherit the polluted isAdmin=true and polluted='PROTOTYPE_POLLUTED' properties from the Object prototype.
verification_method: The exploit script checks: (1) empty objects inherit polluted properties, (2) Object.prototype shows the polluted properties, (3) specific values isAdmin=true and polluted='PROTOTYPE_POLLUTED' match the payload. This distinguishes prototype pollution from normal property assignment.
evidence_produced_by_run: testObject.isAdmin === true confirms newly created objects inherit polluted isAdmin property
Object.prototype.isAdmin === true confirms Object prototype is directly polluted
Malicious plugin successfully used extendContext hook to inject __proto__ keys
Object.assign in orchestrator.ts did not filter dangerous keys (__proto__, constructor)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the main branch, and builds the Docker image.
